channel.title: Comments for page "Excerpt from The Astrolabe Institute Journal"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Excerpt from The Astrolabe Institute Journal"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope! At this point, that particular event has spread. They've been eradicated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Record in SCP 3848 document say dogs are relocated. It was probably just the citizens being told that they are eradicated

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…they were likely not the cause of his illness, considering that most had been eradicated by city governments not long after the interview.

Sweet fuck. This was such a painful read and a brilliant way to demonstrate the dangers SCP-3848 can induce.

+1

Also, am I getting this implication right?
spoiler?????

So it felt pretty clear that SCP-3848 had destroyed the Veil entirely. However, I noticed that the Foundation was at no point directly named, even when groups like the GOC were. Has SCP-3848 caused most people to forget specific details on the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading the sequence with the dictionary was brutal. You did a great job of making it sad and horrifying at the same time. They feel a hole in their brain, I feel a hole in my heart. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this, a lot. I feel like this easily could have been horribly boring/over-technical as many semi-obscure seemingly unimportant anomalies (such as a large group of people just forgetting what dogs are) can often be, but this didn't feel that way at all. I was able to follow what was going on throughout, and the excellent writing really helped make this into a heart-wrenching, intriguing tale. Once again, great stuff from this team.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops. Fixed! Something was deleted in the posting from sandbox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly had no idea what I was reading at any point before looking at the comments, and the rehash of "forgetting what dogs are" thing wasn't terribly interesting.

Dr. F. Johansson: We are a Scientific Journal of the Anomalous, remember?

Mr. Byron: Did you?

Also, this exchange makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
collapsing main narrative spoiler-y post

+ show block
– hide block

But I doubt the town would have been scared, since organizations containing the anomalous have been in the public eye as long as I can remember.

I already told you I love this Broken Masquerade fridge horror in and of itself, but a secondary fridge horror has surfaced, and it's that I just realized you got around my containment procedures1:

In the event that SCP-3848 affects the knowledge of anything conceptually similar to awareness of the Foundation's existence, the knowledge of what is or is not considered anomalous by Foundation standards, and/or the concept of containment, personnel affected are to be transferred out of the affected site(s) and into the care of memetics specialists in other Foundation facilities.

They didn't have a procedure developed for if a Lethe Event took out the knowledge that the Foundation is supposed to be secret. They knew 3848 might eventually target 'what is containing something' or 'who is the Foundation' but they didn't predict this. So everyone's screwed; excellent work.

Footnotes
1. you sly doog, you make a captain proud

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The part about Byron beating his own dog because he didn't recognize it any more put a lump in my throat and a pit in my heart.

item.title: Post of The Author
item.content:encoded:
Part Three of the Doomsday Contest team with WrongJohnSilver, Cyantreuse, and Rimple. Special thanks to the critique from Dr Aers and Taffeta.

The image is from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Borzoi#/media/File:Chart_rosyjski_borzoj_rybnik-kamien_pl.jpg

+ Is this a GoI format?
-  Is this a GoI format?

No. This GoI only exists within the canon of our Contest story. It cannot function within the normal Foundation canon nor should it appear there. This is using the nature of the GoI format to tell a larger story.

+ What's going on here?
-  What's going on here?

The Masquerade has been forgotten. The Foundation still functions at containing, but the oncoming wave of Lethe Events have deleted the concept of the Veil from the boundaries of the UN. The world looks a lot different now, and groups like the Astrolabe Institute can flourish in this faux Broken Masquerade.
